title: cors() configured with origin: "*" — any website can call the API with the user's session

vulnerable.detail: cors() is called with origin: "*", allowing ANY website to make cross-origin requests to this API. Combined with credentials, this lets a malicious page act as the logged-in user. Set origin to an explicit allow-list (a string, array, or function), not "*".

fixed.detail: cors() sets an explicit allow-list origin (not "*") — cross-origin access is restricted to trusted sites.

title: tls.Config{InsecureSkipVerify: true} disables TLS certificate verification (MITM)

vulnerable.detail: tls.Config sets InsecureSkipVerify: true, which turns OFF certificate and host-name verification. Any machine on the path can present a self-signed cert and silently MITM the connection. Remove it (default false); for a private CA set RootCAs instead.

fixed.detail: tls.Config sets InsecureSkipVerify to a safe value — the server's certificate chain and host name are verified.

title: https.request with rejectUnauthorized: false — TLS certificate validation disabled (MITM)

vulnerable.detail: https.request is called with rejectUnauthorized: false, which turns OFF TLS certificate validation. Any machine on the network path can present a self-signed certificate and silently man-in-the-middle the connection — credentials and data are exposed. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; if a private CA is needed, pass `ca` instead.

fixed.detail: https.request keeps certificate validation on (rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the server's TLS identity is verified.
